_meta.name: ALM 14B r10 LoRA training — regression diagnosis

result.verdict: REGRESSED vs r9 lenient baseline (0.8667 → 0.7000, Δ=-0.1667)

comparison.interpretation: r10 LoRA training ON small synthetic hire_sim corpus caused catastrophic forgetting on conversational/instruction-following capability of base Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct. Code + schedule (deterministic outputs) preserved; email/meeting/doc/research (free-form text) regressed.

root_cause.hypothesis_primary: lr=2e-6 × 1500 steps × tiny corpus (100 tasks × ~30 samples) → over-fit to keyword density, lost instruction-following nuance. Final loss 7.82 vs base loss ~2.0 indicates LoRA pushed model far from base distribution. EMA never dipped below 7.8 — model was diverging mildly throughout.
root_cause.hypothesis_secondary: synthetic corpus quality — hire_sim_synthetic_r10 was likely templated/repetitive, causing mode collapse on free-form domains.
root_cause.hypothesis_tertiary: lora_r=16 + alpha=32 on top of already-trained r9 LoRA stacking — effective rank too high, drift compounded.

actions_taken: Reverted live serve (itfl66q4z768kh:8090) to /runpod/ckpt_r9 — best ALM remains r9 lenient
Saved r10 ckpt at /runpod/ckpt_r10 (preserved for reference, not serving)
Created r11 design task — see r11_design section below

r11_design.rationale: Lower lr + fewer steps + smaller LoRA rank + corpus mixing → preserve base capability while nudging toward hire_sim domain. Target: maintain r9 lenient ≥0.85 AND not regress free-form domains.
r11_design.blocked_by: Need pod (current 2 H100s used: serve + CLM r4). Wait for CLM r4 pod or reuse after.

lessons_for_future_runs: Synthetic-only LoRA corpus + non-trivial step count = high regression risk on free-form generation
Track per-domain rate during training (not just loss) — loss is misleading when capability shifts across domains
Stack-on-top-of-existing-LoRA training requires lower lr than from-scratch (2e-6 was already 'low' for vanilla but high for stacking)
Final loss ema > base inference loss (~2.0) is a red flag — model is moving away from base distribution
